content: Lung cancer is the leading cause of cancer death worldwide. Two main types: non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC, 85% of cases) and small cell lung cancer (SCLC, 15%). NSCLC subtypes: adenocarcinoma (most common, often peripheral, associated with EGFR/ALK mutations), squamous cell carcinoma (central, smoking-related, P40/P63 positive), large cell carcinoma (diagnosis of exclusion). SCLC characterized by rapid growth, early metastasis, neuroendocrine features (chromogranin, synaptophysin positive), initially chemo-sensitive but develops resistance. Molecular drivers in NSCLC: EGFR mutations (10-15% Western, 50% Asian, more in never-smokers, women, adenocarcinoma); ALK rearrangements (3-5%); ROS1 (1-2%); BRAF V600E (2-4%); KRAS (25-30%, historically undruggable, now sotorasib/adagrasib available); MET exon 14 skipping (3-4%); RET fusions (1-2%); NTRK fusions (<1%). PD-L1 expression guides immunotherapy use.
